title: AI is not as smart as you think. Here's why
post:
We collectively decided AI was magic after seeing a few viral demos. But spend enough time with these tools and you'll notice the seams: confident wrong answers, inability to reason about novel situations, and a tendency to sound authoritative while being completely off base.

AI is incredibly useful. It's also incredibly limited. The people who understand both truths — who can leverage its power while respecting its boundaries — will always outperform those who swing between blind enthusiasm and paranoid rejection.

Where have you noticed AI falling short of the hype? Thoughts?
